chapter_goal: Make natural gradient and singular learning visible through metric ellipses, descent paths, dual mirror coordinates, Fisher eigenvalue collapse, and plateau diagnostics.
computational_checks: metric SPD and natural direction constrained optimum
monotone Euclidean and natural objective traces
mirror-dual update residual after removing additive log-normalizer
Fisher PSD over the MLP toy grid
artifact existence and nonzero file sizes

library_routing.why: local quadratic geometry is two-dimensional and needs durable labeled inspection

library_routing.representation: contours plus lifted interactive loss surface
library_routing.why: static contours show path geometry; Plotly exposes loss height interactively

library_routing.representation: signed versus absolute Hessian update arrows
library_routing.why: the phenomenon is a 2D eigenvalue sign calculation

library_routing.representation: simplex path and log-ratio coordinate path
library_routing.why: entropy mirror descent lives on a 2-simplex with exact probability checks

library_routing.representation: heatmaps of minimum Fisher eigenvalue and condition number
library_routing.why: one-hidden-unit Fisher matrices can be computed directly over a grid

library_routing.representation: singular loss, slowdown curves, product-prior histogram
library_routing.why: small toy models isolate critical-region behavior without hiding it in a full network

source_span_read.used_for: orientation on section order, terminology, formulas, and concept boundaries only

visual_sequence.inspection_target: metric ellipses and the Fisher-steepest arrow
visual_sequence.validation: metric norm and Cauchy equality for the natural direction

visual_sequence.inspection_target: ordinary and natural paths over the same objective
visual_sequence.validation: both losses descend; natural final loss is smaller

visual_sequence.inspection_target: descent paths lifted to loss height
visual_sequence.validation: same path/loss arrays as the static trajectory check

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Newton versus absolute-Hessian direction at a saddle
visual_sequence.validation: Newton moves toward the saddle in the unstable coordinate while absolute Hessian moves away

visual_sequence.inspection_target: simplex probability path and dual log-ratio path
visual_sequence.validation: probabilities normalize and dual residual is an additive constant

visual_sequence.inspection_target: singular axes where Fisher eigenvalues collapse
visual_sequence.validation: Fisher matrices are PSD and axis eigenvalues are near zero

visual_sequence.inspection_target: flat critical set, plateau slowdown, and singular prior mass
visual_sequence.validation: ordinary slowdown lasts longer; product prior mass spikes near zero
